author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
title: Cœlestius Condemned by Zosimus.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The venerable Pope Zosimus, keeping in view this deprecatory preamble, dealt with the man, puffed up as he was with the blasts of false doctrine, so as that he should condemn all the objectionable points which had been alleged against him by the deacon Paulinus, and that he should yield his assent to the rescript of the Apostolic See which had been issued by his predecessor of sacred memory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The accused man, however, refused to condemn the objections raised by the deacon, yet he did not dare to hold out against the letter of the blessed Pope Innocent; indeed, he went so far as to “promise that he would condemn all the points which the Apostolic See condemned.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Thus the man was treated with gentle remedies, as a delirious patient who required rest; but, at the same time, he was not regarded as being yet ready to be released from the restraints of excommunication.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The interval of two months being granted him, until communications could be received from Africa, a place for recovery was conceded to him, under the mild restorative of the sentence which had been pronounced.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For in truth, if he would have laid aside his vain obstinacy, and be now willing to carry out what he had undertaken, and would carefully read the very letter to which he had replied by promising submission, he would yet come to a better mind.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But after the rescripts were duly issued from the council of the African bishops, there were very good reasons why the sentence should be carried out against him, in strictest accordance with equity.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: What these reasons were you may read for yourselves, for we have sent you all the particulars.
